summary: apps/web/ already has a Base UI navigation-menu wrapper (ui/navigation-menu.tsx) that provides Trigger + Content + Popup + Viewport + Positioner with built-in open/close + slide animations via data-state classes. The current active header (components/site-header.tsx) does NOT use it — it renders plain <button> elements with no dropdown behavior. The legacy components/layout/header.tsx demonstrates exactly how to wire NavigationMenu + framer-motion mobile drawer + Image-thumbnail dropdown items, and is a near-template for what we want to build, only at smaller sizes than the new Figma spec. lucide-react covers every icon we need (one mapping uses `Compass` instead of a custom sparkle/lineage glyph). public/ already has /img1.png, /img2.png, /img3.png usable as quick thumbnails; richer photography lives under public/images/programs/*.jpg and public/images/about/*.jpg.

components.name: NavDropdownTrigger
components.notes: NavigationMenuTrigger already exists and auto-rotates a built-in ChevronDownIcon on open via `group-data-popup-open/navigation-menu-trigger:rotate-180`. But its default styles (rounded-lg, hover:bg-muted, h-9, text-sm) do NOT match the active SiteHeader's HeaderLink (text-subtext-2 text-text-secondary, gap-[7px], opacity hover, no background fill, larger 14px chevron with strokeWidth=2). Build a thin wrapper that imports NavigationMenuTrigger and overrides className to mirror HeaderLink in site-header.tsx (transparent, text-subtext-2 font-medium text-text-secondary, hover:opacity-70). Keep the built-in chevron — do not double up.

components.name: MegaMenu
components.notes: NavigationMenuContent + NavigationMenuPositioner give us positioning, portal, popup chrome (rounded-lg bg-popover shadow ring), entry/exit fade+scale animations, and viewport sizing. The MegaMenu is just the inner layout: a ~3-column grid (`grid grid-cols-3 gap-x-6 gap-y-2 p-6 w-[840px]`) with optional column headers, mapping MegaMenuItem children. No new motion needed — the parent NavigationMenuContent already animates.

components.name: MegaMenuItem
components.source: uses apps/web/src/components/ui/navigation-menu.tsx::NavigationMenuLink as the anchor, icon pattern inspired by ui/highlight-card.tsx (size-[52px] rounded-2xl bg-brand-lite text-text-brand)
components.notes: NavigationMenuLink provides Link semantics + focus ring + hover:bg-muted. Replace the default flex with a 2-column layout: 40-44px square icon tile (rounded-lg bg-brand-lite text-text-brand p-2 — smaller than HighlightCard's 52px because it lives in a dense menu), then a column with title (font-heading text-subtext-2 font-semibold text-text-secondary) + subtitle (text-body-sm text-text-tertiary). Override NavigationMenuLink's default p-2 with p-3 gap-3 items-start to match Figma.

components.name: NavDropdown
components.source: renders inside apps/web/src/components/ui/navigation-menu.tsx::NavigationMenuContent (same primitive as MegaMenu, different inner layout)
components.notes: Standard ~445px-wide panel. Inner layout is a single column of 3 NavDropdownItems (flex flex-col gap-1 p-2 w-[445px]). Reuse NavigationMenuContent — animation and chrome are free. The legacy components/layout/header.tsx::Header already wires this pattern with `<NavigationMenuContent className="w-[300px] p-2">` containing mapped DropdownItem — copy that structure, widen to 445px, swap DropdownItem for NavDropdownItem.

components.name: NavDropdownItem
components.source: apps/web/src/components/layout/header.tsx::DropdownItem (lines 115-143) + thumbnail pattern from apps/web/src/components/ui/program-card.tsx (lines 72-101: relative parent + next/image fill + sizes + object-cover)
components.notes: DropdownItem in legacy header.tsx is 95% of what we need: NavigationMenuLink wrapping a 80x64 thumbnail (next/image fill object-cover) + title + description + course count. To match new Figma spec: bump thumbnail to a larger ratio (likely ~110x88 or 16:10), add a footer-link row (e.g. `View all courses →` with ArrowRight from lucide — already used in program-card.tsx line 4), apply Bodhi tokens (font-heading text-subtext-2 text-text-secondary for title, text-body-sm text-text-tertiary for subtitle, text-brand-primary for footer link). Move out of layout/header.tsx into ui/nav-dropdown-item.tsx so it is reusable.

components.name: site-header integration
components.notes: Active file. Currently HeaderLink (lines 192-222) renders a non-functional <button aria-expanded={false}> when link.hasDropdown is true — zero dropdown behavior wired. Refactor: import NavigationMenu/List/Item from ui/navigation-menu.tsx, wrap the desktop <nav> region (lines 93-100) with <NavigationMenu align="start">, then per link decide: plain Link (Workshops/Our Centers), NavDropdown trigger+content (Teacher Courses / Advanced Certifications / Yoga Courses), or MegaMenu trigger+content (About Us — note: About Us currently has hasDropdown:false in DEFAULT_NAV_LINKS line 35; flip to true and tag with a `kind: 'mega' | 'panel'` discriminator on HeaderNavLink). Keep the existing scroll-shrink, search button, CTA, mobile drawer untouched.

base_ui_navigation_menu.covers: Portal + viewport-mode popup with positioning (side/sideOffset/align), entry+exit animations (fade + scale + horizontal slide between sibling triggers), focus management, ESC/outside-click close, ARIA wiring, and data-state attributes for styling open/closed/transition states.
base_ui_navigation_menu.underlying_lib: @base-ui/react@^1.4.1 (NavigationMenu primitive from @base-ui/react/navigation-menu) — Base UI is the successor to Radix Primitives. The wrapper imports `NavigationMenu as NavigationMenuPrimitive` from `@base-ui/react/navigation-menu`.
base_ui_navigation_menu.animation_built_in: NavigationMenuContent uses data-[motion=from-end|from-start|to-end|to-start] for left/right slides between sibling triggers (52px) + data-starting-style/data-ending-style for fade+scale on open/close. NavigationMenuPopup adds scale-90 + opacity-0 enter/exit. Duration ~0.35s ease cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1).
missing_for_our_use_case: Default NavigationMenuTrigger styles (rounded-lg, hover:bg-muted, text-sm, h-9) do not match HeaderLink's tokens (text-subtext-2, text-text-secondary, opacity hover) — must override className.

Default popup width tracks the popup-width CSS var per content; for MegaMenu and 445px NavDropdown we just set width on the inner div inside NavigationMenuContent (legacy header does this with `w-[300px]`).
No prebuilt 3-column 'mega' layout — we own the inner grid.

Mobile: NavigationMenu is desktop-only in this codebase; the mobile drawer in site-header.tsx is hand-rolled. Mega-menu mobile experience needs to either collapse to accordion sections or fall back to plain Links (legacy header.tsx falls back to plain Links on mobile).
base_ui_navigation_menu.reference_usage_in_repo: apps/web/src/components/layout/header.tsx lines 165-192 — full NavigationMenu + List + Item + Trigger + Content + Link wiring with mapped data, the canonical template.

icons_lucide.package_version_pinned: lucide-react@^1.16.0 in apps/web/package.json (already used heavily — ChevronDown, Menu, Search, X, ArrowRight, etc.)
icons_lucide.notes: All 10 categories have at least one direct lucide-react match; most have a sensible alt. For 'sparkle/lineage' specifically, `Sparkles` is the literal sparkle; `Compass` reads more like 'path/lineage' if the Figma glyph is directional. Confirm against the Figma frame before locking.

image_assets.thumbnail_pattern_in_use:
apps/web/src/components/ui/program-card.tsx lines 72-101: `
`. Same pattern at smaller scale in apps/web/src/components/layout/header.tsx::DropdownItem lines 121-129 (`relative h-16 w-20 shrink-0 overflow-hidden rounded-md`). apps/web/src/components/ui/instructor-card.tsx uses the same pattern for circular avatars.
image_assets.recommendation: For NavDropdownItem thumbnails: use the existing /images/programs/*.jpg photography for the three course-type dropdowns (Teacher Courses, Advanced Certifications, Yoga Courses) — e.g. ttc-300.jpg (online), bala-yoga-teacher-training.jpg (offline/in-studio), face-yoga.jpg or pranayama.jpg (pre-recorded). /images/nav/{online-courses,offline-courses,pre-recorded}.svg already exist and may be intended as icon/illustration accents — check if they're photographic or vector before using as the thumbnail. /img1.png, /img2.png, /img3.png (the legacy placeholders used by components/layout/header.tsx) are small ~23KB images and fine as last-resort fallbacks. For MegaMenu icon tiles use lucide glyphs (not raster images).

motion_usage: apps/web/src/components/layout/header.tsx — mobile drawer height+opacity AnimatePresence (lines 6, 225-274), the closest precedent to what site-header.tsx's mobile section may want

key_tokens_to_use.trigger_text: text-subtext-2 font-medium text-text-secondary, gap-[7px], hover:opacity-70 (mirror HeaderLink in site-header.tsx lines 193-197)
key_tokens_to_use.popup_surface: rounded-lg bg-popover shadow ring-1 ring-foreground/10 (already applied by NavigationMenuPopup in navigation-menu.tsx line 116)
key_tokens_to_use.mega_menu_icon_tile: rounded-lg bg-brand-lite text-text-brand (smaller variant of HighlightCard's size-[52px] rounded-2xl bg-brand-lite)
key_tokens_to_use.dropdown_footer_link: text-brand-primary text-body-sm font-medium hover:opacity-80 + ArrowRight size-3.5 (mirror ProgramCard footer link, program-card.tsx lines 170-179)

open_questions: About Us is currently `hasDropdown: false` in DEFAULT_NAV_LINKS (site-header.tsx line 35). Confirm we flip it to true with a `kind: 'mega'` tag, and that Workshops + Our Centers stay plain Links.
Mobile behaviour for MegaMenu: collapse to a 1-column accordion under the About Us row, or just keep the existing plain Link (current legacy approach)?
Does the Figma frame use Sparkles or Compass for 'lineage'? Both are available in lucide; pick after seeing the glyph.
Are /images/nav/*.svg vector illustrations or icon glyphs? If illustrative they could swap into NavDropdownItem thumbnails (no need for raster); if iconic they belong inside MegaMenuItem tiles.
